Daily Releases from Detention (January 8 – February 24, 2026)"},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_political_prisoner_release_in_Venezuela","title":"2026 political prisoner release in Venezuela - Wikipedia","content":"As of 8 March, 621 political prisoners have been confirmed to be released since its announcement, with over 500 remaining according to the NGO Foro Penal.
Human rights organizations have qualified the releases as a revolving door effect as some prisoners are released, while others are still being arrested for political reasons.
Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado has called for the immediate release of all political prisoners.
As of 24 February, 91 prisoners have been confirmed released under the amnesty law, according to Foro Penal.
El Nacional reported that some of the names of the prisoners out of the 400 released according to Rodríguez were from December 2025 and from 1 January 2026, before the strikes.
On 26 January, Foro Penal confirmed 250 prisoners released since 8 January.
The detainees had been held at Ramo Verde military prison and the National Institute for Women's Orientation (INOF).
We don’t have a date for a fair and free election.
About 30 prisoners were released on 23 February from Rodeo 1.
17 more were released on 8 March.
Biagio Pilieri [es] — Italy-Venezuela — 28 August 2024 — 8 January 2026 (1 year, 4 months and 11 days)
Jesús Armas [es] — Venezuela — 10 December 2024 — 8 February 2026 (1 year, 1 month and 29 days)
Archived from the original on 5 July 2025.
2026 political prisoner release in Venezuela"},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/26/venezuela-frees-104-political-prisoners-rights-group-says","title":"Venezuela frees 104 political prisoners, rights group says | Human Rights News | Al Jazeera","content":"The Caracas-based Foro Penal said at least 104 prisoners were released on Sunday and that the number could rise.
Gonzalo Himiob, Foro Penal’s vice president, said the number of releases was “non-definitive” and could increase.
Rights groups have disputed the government’s figures, with Foro Penal estimating that only about half as many people have been released as claimed by the authorities."},{"url":"https://foropenal.com/articulo/situation-of-political-prisoners-in-venezuela-january-february-2026","title":"Situación de presos políticos en Venezuela enero-febrero 2026 · Foro Penal","content":"17 de marzo, 2026 2 min de lectura 16,782 lecturas
This report summarizes key developments between January 3 and February 24, 2026, based on information collected and verified by our organization, Foro Penal."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Foro Penal Venezuela post July 2024 election detainees count latest report 2025 2026 political prisoners","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://foropenal.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Reporte-EneroFebrero_2026_260313-INGLES-1_compressed.pdf","title":"[PDF] POLITICAL PRISONERS VENEZUELA - Foro Penal","content":"Diseño y diagramación: madominguezv@gmail.com / @TutuDominguez 2026 J A N U A R Y – F E B R U A R Y SITUATION OF POLITICAL PRISONERS IN VENEZUELA POLITICAL CONTEXT On January 3, 2026, a political rupture occurred in Venezuela following the capture and extraction of Nicolás Maduro during a United States military operation conducted in Caracas, referred to as “Operation Absolute Resolve.” 1 Since then, the interim government led by Del-cy Rodríguez has promoted a narrative of reconciliation, announcing large-scale releases of detainees and proposing the adoption of an Amnesty Law. Nevertheless, patterns of repression, institutional mistrust, and judicial opacity continue to persist. CURRENT SITUATION AND VERIFIED DATA According to our records, as of February 24, 2026, more than 573 individuals classified by our organization as political prisoners remain in detention, despite 436 verified releases from detention since January 8, as well as 109 full releases following the entry into force of the Amnesty Law on February 19, 2026.","score":0.91988057,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_political_prisoner_release_in_Venezuela","title":"2026 political prisoner release in Venezuela - Wikipedia","content":"In the aftermath of the 2026 United States strikes in Venezuela and capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores, the remaining government in Venezuela, led by his vice president Delcy Rodríguez as acting president, announced the release of multiple Venezuelan and foreign political prisoners in Venezuela starting on 8 January. As of 8 March, 621 political prisoners have been confirmed to be released since its announcement, with over 500 remaining according to the NGO Foro Penal. Jorge Rodríguez \"Jorge Rodríguez (Venezuelan politician)\"), the brother of Delcy Rodríguez and president of the National Assembly of Venezuela, announced on 8 January 2026 that an \"important\" number of political prisoners would be released as a \"gesture\" by the government. The United Nations Human Rights Council–mandated Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on Venezuela reported that about 50 out of the estimated 800 political prisoners in Venezuela were released as of 12 January, saying it fell short on the Venezuelan government's international human rights obligations, and called for the \"immediate and unconditional\" release of all political prisoners.","score":0.818055,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://foropenal.com/categoria/reportes-de-represion","title":"Reportes de Represión","content":"Foro Penal SITUATION OF POLITICAL PRISONERS IN VENEZUELA JANUARY–FEBRUARY 2026. On January 3, 2026, a political rupture occurred in Venezuela","score":0.8173562,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/26/venezuela-frees-104-political-prisoners-rights-group-says","title":"Venezuela frees 104 political prisoners, rights group says","content":"Authorities in Venezuela have freed more than 100 people listed as political prisoners, The Caracas-based Foro Penal said at least 104","score":0.78413767,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://kennedyhumanrights.org/press/new-report-analyzes-increasing-political-repression-in-venezuela-following-2024-presidential-elections","title":"New report analyzes increasing political repression in Venezuela following 2024 presidential elections - Robert & Ethel Kennedy Human Rights Center","content":"## New report analyzes increasing political repression in Venezuela following 2024 presidential elections. Kennedy Human Rights and Foro Penal today published a new report outlining patterns of political repression in Venezuela following the July 2024 presidential elections, and the 2025 parliamentary and regional elections. The report, titled *Electoral Repression, Enforced Disappearances and Political Control in Venezuela*, analyzes how President Nicolas Maduro’s regime has increasingly utilized enforced disappearances and hostage-taking to silence political opposition, ensure domestic control, and barter with other countries. Building upon two prior joint reports in 2020 and 2022, this new report examines how enforced disappearance continues to be deployed as part of the Maduro regime’s evolving criminal policy, specifically in relation to the three most recent election processes: the July 2024 presidential election, the May 2025 parliamentary and gubernatorial elections, and the July 2025 municipal election. In light of this continued and systemic repression from the Maduro regime, RFK Human Rights and Foro Penal highlight the need for sustained accountability efforts at the national, regional, and international levels.","score":0.76888984,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.56,"request_id":"0e1a30ce-382b-43ad-9226-6bb3a246c505"}
